category_title: Preschoolers engaging in exclusive, manipulative, and rule-breaking play
minimal_context: Delia is playing a new game in the coat and boot closet during free play.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Delia, I see you are in the coat and boot closet. What are you doing?

STUDENT: I took up a new game in here.

TUTOR: Tucker is walking over to the closet now.

STUDENT: I am finding new ways to control this space.

TUTOR: Tucker is asking if he can play the game with you.

STUDENT: (Mimicking a firm, but caring tone) I’m sorry, but only two are allowed in this game right now. I can tell you are feeling sad. Try again later.

options.A: Complete consulting notes on a daily basis to share information and strategies regarding the student's play with other staff members.
options.B: Use colorful and varied visual materials in the coat and boot closet during the free play period.
options.C: Maintain a 'leaky' orientation to control, avoiding inflexible regimes of disciplinary control where children are assessed for 'time on task'.
options.D: Model behavior using the pre-packaged plush hand-puppets with the student during the free play period.
